title: Coconut-Sweet Norwegian Unwaxed Lime Bowl
description: A sweet and refreshing glassy-jelly-like dessert featuring unwaxed lime and coconut cream crack, perfect for a date night treat.
instructions: Zest the unwaxed limes and reserve for later use.
Cut the limes in half and juice them, straining the juice to remove seeds and pulp.
In a small saucepan, combine the coconut cream, granulated sugar, and salt. Heat over medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the sugar dissolves and the mixture comes to a simmer.
Remove the saucepan from the heat and stir in the butter until melted.
Let the mixture cool slightly, then fold in the egg whites until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a baking dish or a deep-dish pie plate and smooth the top.
Bake at 350°F (175°C) for 25-30 minutes, or until the edges are set and the center is slightly jiggly.
Cool the mixture to room temperature, then chill in the refrigerator for at least 2 hours.
Zest the unwaxed limes and sprinkle over the top of the chilled mixture.
Dust with confectioners' sugar and serve in lacquerware bowls, garnished with toasted coconut flakes if desired.
Slice the heavy cream and serve on the side, if desired.
